# PIOS — Personal Intelligent Operating System
# VeritasIQ Technologies Ltd | pios-wysskz48mv-engs-projects.vercel.app
# llms.txt v1.0 — Claude for Chrome integration manifest

## What is PIOS?

PIOS is a personal AI operating system for multi-domain professionals. It unifies academic
research management, FM consulting delivery, SaaS business operations, task management,
calendar, email triage, expense tracking, payroll, and meeting notes into a single
Claude-powered platform.

Primary user: Douglas Masuku — DBA candidate (University of Portsmouth), Group CEO of
Sustain International FZE Ltd, FM consultant, SaaS founder (VeritasEdge™, InvestiScript).

## Key capabilities Claude can help with

- Review and create tasks across domains (academic, fm_consulting, saas, business, personal)
- Check and generate the AI morning brief
- Sync email inboxes and triage unread messages
- Log and categorise expenses with AI auto-categorisation
- Create meeting notes and extract action items from transcripts
- Check thesis chapter word counts and academic module deadlines
- Review payroll runs and approve transfers
- Access cross-platform live data (VeritasEdge, InvestiScript, GitHub)
- Check research journals and call-for-papers deadlines

## Authentication

PIOS uses Supabase Auth (Google OAuth). Users must be signed in at the platform URL.
Claude for Chrome shares the browser session — no separate login needed if already signed in.

## Platform routes

### Core navigation
GET /platform/dashboard       — Command centre with AI brief, tasks, calendar, cross-platform KPIs
GET /platform/tasks           — Task list with domain filters, priority, AI prioritisation
GET /platform/calendar        — Calendar events with AI pre-meeting briefs
GET /platform/meetings        — Meeting notes, transcript ingestion, AI action extraction
GET /platform/email           — Inbox intelligence, multi-account triage
GET /platform/academic        — DBA modules, thesis chapters, supervision sessions
GET /platform/research        — Academic database search, journal watchlist, CFP tracker
GET /platform/expenses        — Expense tracker with AI categorisation and CSV export
GET /platform/payroll         — Payroll runs, expense claims, bank transfer queue
GET /platform/files           — File intelligence and invoice tracking
GET /platform/projects        — Active projects dashboard
GET /platform/command         — Live data: VeritasEdge, InvestiScript, GitHub
GET /platform/ai              — PIOS AI companion (free-form chat with full context)
GET /platform/settings        — Profile, email accounts, integrations

### API endpoints Claude can call directly
GET  /api/claude-context              — Structured live context (tasks, brief, stats) — primary endpoint
GET  /api/tasks?domain=&status=       — Task list with filters
POST /api/tasks                       — Create task: {title, domain, priority, due_date, description}
PATCH /api/tasks                      — Update task: {id, status, priority, due_date}
GET  /api/brief                       — Today's AI morning brief (or generate if missing)
POST /api/email/sync                  — Trigger inbox sync for all connected accounts
GET  /api/email/accounts              — List connected email accounts
POST /api/expenses { action:'create'} — Log expense: {description, amount, currency, category, domain, date}
POST /api/expenses { action:'ai_categorise'} — AI-suggest category from description
GET  /api/expenses?domain=&limit=     — List expenses with summary stats
POST /api/meetings                    — Create meeting note: {title, raw_transcript, auto_process:true}
GET  /api/meetings?domain=            — List meeting notes
POST /api/meetings { action:'promote_tasks', id } — Promote action items to tasks
GET  /api/academic                    — Academic modules, thesis chapters, supervision sessions
GET  /api/calendar                    — Calendar events
GET  /api/notifications               — Unread notifications count and list
GET  /api/health                      — Platform health check

## Suggested Claude for Chrome workflows

### Morning routine
1. GET /api/claude-context to see today's brief, urgent tasks, and calendar
2. GET /api/email/sync to pull latest emails from all inboxes
3. Review high-priority items and create follow-up tasks via POST /api/tasks

### After a meeting
1. Navigate to /platform/meetings
2. Create a new meeting note with the transcript pasted in
3. POST /api/meetings with auto_process:true — Claude extracts action items
4. POST /api/meetings with action:promote_tasks to add items to task list

### Expense logging
1. POST /api/expenses with action:ai_categorise to get category suggestion
2. POST /api/expenses with action:create to log the expense
3. GET /api/expenses to review with summary stats

### Weekly review
1. GET /api/claude-context for full platform snapshot
2. GET /api/tasks?status=overdue to see overdue items
3. GET /api/meetings for recent meetings and pending action items

## Data structure hints

### Task object
{ id, title, description, domain, priority (critical|high|medium|low),
  status (todo|in_progress|done|cancelled), due_date, source, created_at }

### Expense object
{ id, description, amount, currency, category, domain, date, billable, client, notes }

### Meeting note object
{ id, title, meeting_date, meeting_type, domain, attendees[], ai_summary,
  ai_action_items[], ai_decisions[], status (draft|processed|reviewed) }

## Safety notes for Claude for Chrome

- Do not submit payroll transfers without explicit user confirmation
- Do not delete files or archive tasks without user approval
- Expenses and tasks created automatically should be marked with source note
- Email drafts should be reviewed before sending
- All financial data is user-specific and RLS-protected — only the signed-in user's data is accessible
